message:
fix(parser): always register subquery aliases in scope even when unnamed

Previously, the resolver only called `scope.add_alias` for subqueries
that carried an explicit AS alias. When a subquery appeared without an
alias (e.g., `SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1)`), no alias was registered at
all, which meant downstream resolution stages could not find the
subquery's contribution to the FROM scope. Depending on the query shape
this could lead to "no such column" false positives or missing scope
entries during column resolution.

Change the logic to unconditionally call `scope.add_alias`, using
`alias.as_deref().unwrap_or("")` to provide an empty-string fallback
for unaliased subqueries. This mirrors the existing pattern used by
`TableFunction` on the very next match arm, which already falls back to
the function name. The empty alias still gets registered in the scope
table so the resolver knows a subquery source exists at that position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
